new_tags.definition: Grouped (multi-series) bars reduced/re-encoded to single-series simple bars — e.g. filtering a grouped bar chart to one series/year collapses the grouping into plain bars on a rebuilt scale.
new_tags.first_seen: e5_q3.js function1 (l343-352, renderDietSimpleBarChart over Year===2019 subset)

new_tags.definition: A brand-new auxiliary chart built by isolating a RAW subset of the source data (not re-encoding the full dataset, and not showing computed/derived values as materialized_chart does).
new_tags.first_seen: e6_q7.js:204-256 renderRaceFocusChart — rebuilds container as a 2-bar chart of just White/Black 'Almost all of the time' raw percentages.

new_tags.definition: An encoding transpose that swaps which field maps to the category/x axis versus the series/color grouping, restructuring the same data under a new grouping (distinct from stack<->group or line->bar re-encodings).

A highlight-colored stroke drawn ON TOP OF a contiguous subset of an existing data line to emphasize that segment (traces the data path, unlike reference/local_reference lines which mark a computed level).
new_tags.first_seen: e1_q4.js:217-231 (function1 — RISE_COLOR paths over each increasing run)

new_tags.definition: Existing marks animate their position (x/y) to a re-sorted or re-centered order with the axis re-called, with no data removed and no color/size change as the salient effect (a pure reordering position tween).
new_tags.first_seen: e1_q6.js:161-184 (function1 — bars slide into ascending-sorted scaleBand positions)

new_tags.definition: A per-category glyph/symbol (e.g. ▲ vs ·) placed above each category to mark which categories pass a boolean/comparison test, distinct from a numeric text label or an opacity highlight.
A set of evenly-spaced horizontal (or vertical) grid reference lines spanning the plot at axis-tick positions to aid value reading — scaffolding, not a single computed threshold/avg/median line.

A background rectangle filling the plot area on one side of a horizontal reference/threshold line (e.g. the whole region below the average) to shade all values beyond that threshold — a y-axis value-region tint, as opposed to window_shading's x-axis category/time span.

Existing marks smoothly interpolate their GEOMETRY (x/width/y/height, cx/cy, or path `d` via attrTween) to a new scale or layout — a positional/shape tween of already-present elements, distinct from transition_grow (baseline growth entrance), transition_fade (opacity), and transition_recolor (color/opacity in place).
new_tags.first_seen: e2_q2.js:379-408 (D3 update-selection .transition() morphing bar x/width/y/height to new scale); also e2_q9.js:298-308 line path attrTween and e2_q10.js:359-375.

new_tags.definition: A full-height vertical (or full-width horizontal) line that DIVIDES the plot into labeled regions/groups (e.g. a year boundary), not encoding a threshold/average value like reference_line.
new_tags.first_seen: e10_q4.js:348-356 (function2 year-split divider at the Dec'20/Jan'21 midpoint with 2020/2021 region labels)
